text.rule: Fill the kill-criteria template BEFORE the launch event; a template filled after launch is invalid and must be discarded.
Name exactly one primary kill metric (e.g. MRR, weekly signups, paying customers, joy-score) and one threshold value with a deadline date — vague clauses like "if it's not growing" are rejected.

Set at least three review checkpoints from launch (T+14d, T+30d, T+60d) with the metric value required at each; missing two consecutive checkpoints triggers immediate kill review, not silent extension.
Include a "founder joy score" 1-10 self-rating at each checkpoint; if joy drops below 4 for two checkpoints in a row, escalate to kill review regardless of revenue.
Cap spend before next checkpoint: name the maximum dollars and hours allowed; if exceeded, the bet is auto-frozen pending review even if metrics are on track.
Sign the template (digital signature, calendar invite, or commit hash) on day-of-launch and link it from the project README so future-you cannot quietly delete it.
Kill review must produce one of three written outcomes: KILL (sunset playbook starts), CONTINUE (new thresholds set), PIVOT (new bet card, old one closed); "wait and see" is not a valid outcome.
text.example: Indie hacker launches paid Notion template on PH. Kill template: primary metric = $200 MRR by T+60d; checkpoints at T+14 ($50), T+30 ($100), T+60 ($200); joy floor = 4; spend cap = $300 and 30h before next review. T+30 actual = $35 MRR, joy = 3 — two checkpoint misses + joy floor breached → KILL outcome, sunset playbook triggers, founder rotates to next bet.
